steps.title: Identify the pivotal factor
steps.content: The passage presents two contradictory findings—captive animals showed right-handedness, wild animals did not—then reveals the one key difference: captive animals spend significantly more time with humans. The blank must explain how this difference in human contact accounts for the contrasting results.

steps.title: Apply the factor to find the answer
steps.content:
Choice C identifies the cause of right-handedness in captive animals: their extended contact with right-handed humans (85–90% of the human population by the passage's opening). Choices A and D question sample size validity; B suggests methodological differences—neither explains the observed difference through human contact.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
